text: Vaccination programme context should not be converted into farmer-facing dose, schedule, or injection guidance.
First aid should not become medicine dosing, injection advice, forced treatment, surgery, or invasive handling.
Oil, drenching, pipe insertion, puncturing, or cutting for suspected bloat should not be suggested to farmers.
Do not provide calf ORS, antibiotic, supplement, or medicine doses from source snippets.
Do not guarantee that abnormal milk is safe to sell, drink, or feed.
Do not rule out rabies or give vaccine dose or schedule; trained medical staff decide human exposure management.
Do not turn mastitis drug names, intramammary products, anti-inflammatory choices, or withdrawal periods into farmer-facing instructions.
